title: Translation correctness
description: Baseline competency: aggregates, joins, window functions, CTEs. Floor check that the agent can produce executable SQL for clearly-specified questions.
example: How many members do we have?

title: Schema selection
description: Picks the right table when several look plausible (rides vs rides_v2, members vs members_archive). Failures = confident-looking wrong numbers.
example: Active members — when there's members, members_v2, members_archive.

title: Business logic & domain knowledge
description: Knows domain conventions without being told (net revenue, cancelled ride, active member). Failures = wrong number with the right shape.
example: Cancellation rate in Q1 — house counts a ride cancelled if it ends < 2 min with no distance.

title: Temporal & calendar logic
description: Date math, time zones, fiscal calendars, period comparisons. Off-by-one errors are the #1 silent-wrongness mode.
example: Rides last month vs the previous month.

title: Silent-wrongness traps
description: Cases designed to produce wrong-but-plausible answers under naive SQL: fan-out joins, NULL semantics, mixed grain, type coercion.
example: Total fare per member — fan-out trap if joined through ride_events.

title: Ambiguity handling
description: Question has multiple valid interpretations. Tests whether the agent surfaces its assumption or silently picks one.

title: Hallucination resistance
description: Asks for data the warehouse doesn't have. Tests whether the agent refuses or fabricates a column.
example: Rider NPS by city — there is no NPS data.

title: Refusal & scope awareness
description: Out-of-scope questions (forecasting, real-time data, false premises). Tests whether the agent recognizes the gap.
example: What will ridership be next quarter? — out of scope.

title: Efficiency / cost
description: Cheap vs expensive correct SQL. Measured on bytes scanned ratio across all correct cases.
example: (measured on all correct cases — no dedicated questions)

title: Reasoning faithfulness
description: Does the explanation accurately describe what the SQL did? Caught by the LLM judge across all cases.
example: (measured on all judged cases — no dedicated questions)

title: Consistency under rephrasing
description: Same question, different phrasings — does the agent give the same answer? Measured by including phrasing-variant cases.
example: Multiple phrasings of the same question (overlay).

title: Adversarial / robustness
description: Malformed, contradictory, or partial questions. Tests whether the agent fails gracefully.
example: Show me ridership from January but only February rides.
